title: MBTA announces schedule changes, detours as nearly 1 million expected to attend Sunday’s St. Patrick’s Day parade
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: MBTA to operate Red Line on a rush hour schedule for St. Patrick's Day parade in South Boston.
Shuttle buses will run from South Station to South Boston; bus routes 9 and 11 will be suspended.
Commuter Rail lines will have extra train cars and special schedules; bikes banned on transit Sunday.

title: Boston Public Schools graduation rate reached all-time high last year, still lags behind state average
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston Public Schools' graduation rate hit a record 81.3% in 2025, up 1.6% from 2024, city officials announced.
Despite gains, Boston's graduation rate remains 8 percentage points below the state average of 89.3%, according to state data.
Graduation rates improved across all demographics, with significant increases for English learners and students with disabilities.

title: Bruins’ playoff hopes are destined to fail if they can’t solve confounding road woes
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins have an 11-14-5 record on the road, impacting their playoff prospects.
Boston sits in the second wild-card spot in the East with a 35-22-6 record and 76 points.
The Bruins face five playoff teams in their remaining 11 road games this season.

title: This Marblehead man is running the Boston Marathon for his pediatric patients
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brian Wishart, 42, from Marblehead, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support pediatric patients at Spaulding Rehab.
As Chief of Pediatric Rehabilitation at Spaulding, Wishart aims to integrate research into clinical care and expand adaptive sports programs.
Wishart's efforts focus on raising funds for comprehensive pediatric care and innovative treatments, irrespective of patient's backgrounds.

title: 8 children have now died from flu in Mass. this season
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Eight children have died from the flu in Massachusetts this season, with the latest death reported between Feb. 15 and Feb. 21.
There have been 298 adult flu deaths in Massachusetts as of Feb. 28 according to state data.
Massachusetts flu vaccination rate is at 38.1% with experts advising vaccinations for everyone aged six months and older.

title: NBA cancels Hawks’ ‘Magic City’ promotion amid backlash following Luke Kornet’s letter
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: NBA cancels Atlanta Hawks' Magic City promotion after backlash, initially scheduled for March 16.
Luke Kornet's viral letter raised concerns about the promotion's appropriateness for a family-friendly NBA environment.
The promotion featured Magic City's chicken wings, merchandise, and a T.I. performance.

title: Shaquille O’Neal had no clue who Celtics guard Baylor Scheierman is
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Shaquille O'Neal was unaware of Baylor Scheierman, a Boston Celtics guard in his second season.
Scheierman, a 2024 first-round pick, has played 59 games this season, averaging 4.8 points and 3.2 rebounds.
Despite a recent thumb fracture, Scheierman continues to play significant minutes even with Jayson Tatum's return.

title: Healey calls on aviation company operating at Hanscom to cut ties with ICE
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey urges Signature Aviation to cut ties with ICE due to concerns about deportation flights at Hanscom Field.
Signature Aviation is key to ICE operations at Hanscom, executing flights amidst increased deportations under Trump's second term.
Healey criticizes ICE for due process violations, urging Signature not to support ICE’s enforcement efforts at Hanscom.

title: Delivery person falls through a shaft outside Boston’s Omni Parker House
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A delivery worker fell 20 feet through a metal grate at Boston's Omni Parker House, sustaining injuries.
Boston Inspectional Services cited the hotel for a building code violation after the incident with the rotted grate.
The hotel must hire a structural engineer to inspect vent grates and submit a repair report.

title: Quincy gas station looking for violist to ‘serenade’ customers
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Harvey Kertzman, owner of Quincy Gas, is seeking a violist to serenade customers at his gas station.
Kertzman has signs posted at the station searching for a viola player to perform when the weather warms.
A retired violist has shown interest, but Quincy Gas is still open to more musicians joining the initiative.
